items.title: Why Gulf States Are Avoiding Military Retaliation Against Iran
items.desc: As regional tensions intensify, Gulf governments are prioritizing economic stability, diplomatic flexibility, and stronger collective defense instead of direct military confrontation with Iran.

items.title: Kuwait Reports Casualties and Damage Following Iranian Attacks
items.desc: Kuwait said Iranian attacks injured oil sector employees and damaged critical infrastructure, while Iran's Revolutionary Guard claimed responsibility for a separate strike on a US air base in Jordan.

items.title: ASYCUDA Implementation Nears Final Stage, KRG Rejects Salary Link
items.desc: KRG says ASYCUDA customs reform is separate from public-sector salary payments as Erbil and Baghdad move closer to final approval of the system.

items.title: Erbil and Jalal Talabani International Airports Remain Operational Despite Regional Tensions
items.desc: Erbil and Jalal Talabani International airports remain fully operational despite regional missile attacks, with officials confirming open airspace and normal flight operations.

items.title: Ex-U.S. Amb.: Premature Troop Withdrawal Could Leave Iraq Facing Security Vacuum
items.desc: The former US ambassador to Iraq told Kurdistan24 that any American troop withdrawal should be guided by security conditions, warning that a premature exit would not serve Iraq, the Kurdistan Region, or the United States.

items.title: Fuel tanker erupts in flames in Al-Sulaymaniyah
items.desc: Shafaq News- Al-Sulaymaniyah A fuel tanker caught fire in Tasluja in Al-Sulaymaniyah, the Kurdistan Region of Iraq, on Saturday, sending a thick cloud of black smoke into the sky, a local official told Shafaq News. Aram Ali, spokesperson for Al-Sulaymaniyah's Civil Defense Directorate, said the authorities have not yet determined the cause of the fire and the extent of damage. No casualties have b

items.title: Iran signals broader attacks on regional energy infrastructure
items.desc: Shafaq News- Tehran Iran has begun implementing a plan that could extend its response to regional energy, water purification, and desalination infrastructure, including in Kuwait, if US strikes continue, an Iranian military source cited by Middle East Spectator claimed on Saturday. Tehran had also shifted to high-precision medium-range ballistic missiles after earlier launches of less accurate pro

items.title: US Dollar edges lower in Baghdad and Erbil
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad/ Erbil The US dollar closed Saturday’s trading lower in Iraq, hovering around 153,000 dinars per 100 dollars. According to Shafaq News market survey, the dollar traded in Baghdad's Al-Kifah and Al-Harithiya exchanges at 152,950 dinars per 100 dollars, compared to the morning session’s 153,000. In the Iraqi capital, exchange shops sold the dollar at 153,500 dinars a

items.title: Ramadi landfill blazes threaten Iraq’s groundwater quality
items.desc: Shafaq News- Al-Anbar Repeated fires at a landfill south of Ramadi in Iraq’s Al-Anbar province have sent thick smoke over parts of the city, with environmental specialists expressing concerns over deteriorating air quality and possible health threats for residents. The cause of the fires at the sanitary landfill behind Al-Razi Hospital remains unclear, with accusations circulating between wa

items.title: Iraq FA joins FIFA meeting at New York’s Trump Tower
items.desc: Shafaq News- New York The Iraq Football Association participated in a meeting between FIFA executives and national federations at Trump Tower in New York, attended by FIFA President Gianni Infantino, US President Donald Trump, and former football stars. Iraq was represented by IFA First Vice President Sarmad Abdul Ilah and foreign relations adviser Ghaith Muhanna during the closing days of the 202

items.title: Trump threatens new Canada tariffs over fires sending 'filthy' air into US cities
items.desc: Canadian leader Mark Carney says both the US and Canada have an equal responsibility to fight climate change, which experts say are worsening wildfire conditions.

items.title: What this desert city can teach the world about tackling heat deaths
items.desc: Maricopa County in Arizona has some of the hottest temperatures, but it has reduced the number of heat-related deaths.

items.title: He made history in Congress - then the Supreme Court changed its mind
items.desc: Residents say they're afraid that if Shomari Figures loses his seat, their federal-funding could disappear with him.

items.title: Toronto, New York and Washington top list of most polluted cities as wildfires burn
items.desc: Wildfires in Ontario have blanketed US and Canadian cities with dangerous smoke.

items.title: How the World Cup made an 'NFL-obsessed' American a football fan
items.desc: New fans tell the BBC they are fully invested after watching the World Cup on home soil, just don’t ask them to stop calling it soccer.

items.title: Why is it so hard for the U.S. to win wars?
items.desc: U.S. presidents have promised short, decisive wars in Afghanistan, Iraq and Iran. All have proved much more difficult than advertised and fallen far short of the political goals set at the beginning.

items.title: 8 killed and more than 60 wounded in Ukrainian drone attack on Russian regions
items.desc: Kyiv's forces are continuing their aerial campaign against energy infrastructure and military targets inside Russia, aiming to undermine Moscow's war effort.

items.title: The U.S. and Iran blow past red lines as they lurch back toward all-out war
items.desc: The United States and Iran exchanged strikes aimed at infrastructure and military targets on Saturday as their battle over the Strait of Hormuz intensified.

items.title: China signals possible return of U.S. trade privileges for Hong Kong
items.desc: The decision comes two months after President Donald Trump met with Xi Jinping, potentially warming ties ahead of Xi's expected U.S. visit.

items.title: The U.S.-Iran battle over the Strait of Hormuz raises risks for global waterways
items.desc: Recognizing Iran's control over the Strait of Hormuz could set a dangerous precedent, with other countries attempting to claim important waterways, analysts say.

items.title: US strikes hit Iran for seventh consecutive night
items.desc: Iran has retaliated against US allies in the region, with Kuwait reporting that a power and water plant had been hit.

items.title: US strikes oil tanker with missiles as it enforces new Iran blockade
items.desc: The US military targeted an unladen oil tanker which it said was heading for the key Iranian oil terminal on Kharg island.

items.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran as Trump warns Tehran it 'better behave'

items.title: Trump threatens to bomb bridges and power plants unless Iran resumes talks
